data.title: PostNL N.V. GAAP EPS of €0.096, revenue of €937M; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• PostNL N.V. press release (OTCPK:TNTFF): Q4 GAAP EPS of €0.096.
  • Revenue of €937M (+5.4% Y/Y).
  • €0.07 dividend per share to be proposed at AGM.

data.title: Indivior gets FDA approval for label changes for Sublocade injection
data.description:
• Indivior (NASDAQ:INDV) Monday said that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration has approved label changes for Sublocade including a rapid initiation protocol and alternative injection sites, marking a significant advancement in the treatment of moderate to severe opioid use disorder.
  • The rapid initiation protocol reduces time to treatment with Sublocade from one week to one hour, a significant advancement in the treatment of moderate to severe opioid use disorder (OUD).
  • Additionally, the ability to select a different injection site may provide patients more flexibility so that they may be inclined to continue their treatment, the company added.

data.title: Orezone Gold announces C$35M bought deal offering
data.description:
• Orezone Gold (OTCQX:ORZCF) said on Tuesday that it has entered into an agreement with Canaccord Genuity in connection with a "bought deal" private placement of 42.68M common shares of the company at a price of C$0.82 per share for aggregate gross proceeds of C$35M.
  • The company will give the underwriter an option to buy up to 6.40 million additional common shares (the "over-allotment option") at the offering price, for extra gross proceeds of up to C$5.25 million, which can be exercised fully or partially anytime within 30 days after the closing date.
  • The company intends to use the aggregate net proceeds of the offerings to accelerate both the Stage II hard rock expansion and additional exploration at its Bomboré Gold Mine, as well as for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about March 13, 2025.

data.title: Equinox Gold to buy Calibre Mining to create major Americas-focused gold producer
data.description:
Equinox Gold (NYSE:EQX) to acquire all the issued and outstanding common shares of Calibre Mining (OTCQX:CXBMF) in a court-approved plan of arrangement, the companies said on Sunday.

Calibre shareholders will receive 0.31 Equinox common shares for each Calibre share held immediately before the effective time of the transaction, with existing Equinox shareholders and former Calibre shareholders owning approximately 65% and 35% of the combined company upon completion of the deal.

The implied market capitalization of the combined company is estimated at C$7.7 billion.

The transaction is expected to create an Americas-focused diversified gold producer with a portfolio of operating mines in five countries anchored by two high-quality, long-life, low-cost Canadian gold mines.

With 100% ownership of Greenstone and Valentine, New Equinox Gold will become the second-largest gold producer in Canada, the companies said.

The combined company is expected to produce approximately 950,000 ounces of gold in 20251, not including production from Valentine or Los Filos.

"This merger represents a transformative step forward for both Equinox and Calibre, bringing together two complementary companies with strong production, growth potential, operational expertise, and a shared commitment to responsible mining," Greg Smith, President and Chief Executive Officer of Equinox (NYSE:EQX) said.

data.title: European markets mixed amid uncertainty over Trump's moves, geopolitics
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.07%

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.09%

France (CAC:IND) -0.55%

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) eased -0.2% on Monday as investors assessed heightened geopolitical uncertainty in the region, while U.S. President Donald Trump's policies on tariffs and immigration remain in focus.

Trump will meet French and UK leaders as uncertainty grows about U.S. ties to Europe and Ukraine.

Meanwhile, Friedrich Merz, leader of the conservative alliance that won Germany's national election on Sunday, is set to become chancellor, and he has made one thing clear: his focus will be achieving "real independence" from the U.S.

On the corporate front, European food delivery giant Just Eat Takeaway.com is poised to be acquired by Dutch technology investor Prosus in an all-cash deal worth roughly 4.1 billion euros ($4.3 billion).

There are no major earnings on Monday.

Coming in the session: Investors will assess Eurozone inflation data coming up shortly.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 1 basis point to 4.43%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 2.47%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point at 4.58%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: Elon Musk’s DOGE laying off workers at the auto safety agency overseeing Tesla - report
data.description:
The Elon Musk-run Department of Government Efficiency is cutting jobs at the vehicle safety agency that oversees Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and has launched investigations into deadly crashes involving his EV company's cars, the Associated Press reported.

The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration has cut a “modest” number of positions, according to a statement from the agency. Musk has accused NHTSA of holding back progress on self-driving technology with its investigations and recalls, the report said.

The job cuts at NHTSA, enacted by Musk's advisory group, were earlier reported by The Washington Post.

In addition to investigations into Tesla’s partially automated vehicles, NHTSA has mandated that Tesla and other carmakers using self-driving technology report crash data on vehicles, a requirement that Tesla has criticized and that watchdogs fear could be eliminated, the AP report said.

“We have retained positions critical to the mission of saving lives, preventing injuries, and reducing economic costs due to road traffic crashes,” the statement said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Motorola Solutions acquires 911 solution provider RapidDeploy
data.description:
• Motorola Solutions (NYSE:MSI) has acquired RapidDeploy, a cloud-native 911 solution provider based in Austin, Texas.

  • The financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

  • With the development, Motorola Solutions plans to integrate RapidDeploy's technology into its VESTA 911 call handling software and AI-powered VESTA NXT platform which aims to improve public safety agencies' access to data and situational awareness during emergencies.

  • RapidDeploy's Next Generation 911 (NG911) product suite features Radius Mapping for real-time call location, Lightning mobile app for first responders to access 911 data, and Eclipse Analytics for evaluating response times and staffing. It supports over 1,600 PSAPs in 24 states and integrates with Motorola's Rave suite.

More on Motorola Solutions

data.title: Germany's next chancellor Merz calls for 'real independence' from Trump's U.S.
data.description:
Friedrich Merz, leader of the conservative alliance that won Germany's national election on Sunday, is set to become chancellor and he has made one thing clear: his focus will be achieving "real independence" from the U.S.

"My absolute priority will be to strengthen Europe as quickly as possible so that, step by step, we can really achieve independence from the U.S.," Merz said in a televised roundtable. "I never thought I would have to say something like this on a TV program. But after Donald Trump's statements last week, it is clear that the Americans - at least (in) this administration - are largely indifferent to the fate of Europe."

Merz also cast doubts on the NATO summit in June, if the alliance would still be viewed "in its current form" or if independent European defense capabilities need to be established more quickly.

Last week, Merz said Europe must be prepared for the possibility that Trump would not uphold NATO's mutual defense commitment, and pitched the idea of nuclear cooperation with France and the U.K.

Over in the U.S., Trump welcomed the election result. "Much like the USA, the people of Germany got tired of the no common sense agenda, especially on energy and immigration, that has prevailed for so many years," he declared. "This is a great day for Germany, and for the USA under the leadership of a gentleman named Donald J. Trump."

The center-right Christian Democratic Union/Christian Social Union alliance won 28.6% of the vote, while the far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) secured 20.8% - the strongest showing for a far-right party since World War II, the Associated Press reported.

To note, Trump adviser Elon Musk had endorsed the AfD, which Merz said was unique. "The interventions from Washington were no less dramatic and drastic, and ultimately outrageous than the interventions we have seen from Moscow," the incoming chancellor said during the roundtable.

Now, focus will shift to negotiations to form the next coalition government. The most likely outcome is a coalition between CDU/CSU and the Social Democrats (SPD) - previously led by outgoing chancellor Olaf Scholz. The SPD won 16.3% of the vote.

The main focus will be the contentious debt brake, which limits how much debt the government can take on. "A grand coalition (CDU/CSU and SPD), alongside a two-thirds majority needed for the debt-brake reforms, would likely be euro-positive," Morgan Stanley noted.

The euro (EUR:USD) rose 0.4% to $1.05 overnight, while DAX futures were up 1.1%.

data.title: Kosmos Energy reports mixed Q4 results; initiates Q1 and FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Kosmos Energy press release (NYSE:KOS): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.03 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $397.66M (-21.7% Y/Y) beats by $4.26M.
  • Net Production: ~66,800 barrels of oil equivalent per day (boepd), with sales of ~65,700 boepd.
  • Net capital expenditure for the fourth quarter of 2024 was $117 million.
  • The company generated net cash provided by operating activities of approximately $176 million and free cash flow of approximately $14 million in the fourth quarter.
  • In 2025 capital expenditure is expected to be $400 million or below, more than 50% lower than the previous year as we prioritize cash generation through disciplined capital allocation. Additionally, as a part of this ongoing rigorous cost management, the company is targeting a reduction in annual overhead of around $25 million by year-end 2025.

data.title: Globe Telecom tests Nokia’s network exposure platform to enhance security in financial services
data.description:
Philippines-based Globe Telecom is collaborating with Nokia (NYSE:NOK) to provide banks and other enterprises with enhanced security through the utilization of network Application Programming Interfaces, the companies said on Monday.

Globe Telecom, which already uses a host of other Nokia (NYSE:NOK) solutions including 5G RAN, is testing Nokia’s Network Exposure Platform in expanding and simplifying the number of APIs available to the operator and its enterprise partners to enable the creation of security-focused applications.

“With cyberattacks on banking services accelerating, it is crucial that we make available the latest network-powered technologies to our enterprise customers and help them safeguard against fraud,” said Joel Agustin, Globe’s Head of Service Planning and Engineering.
